ail: verb. To cause to suffer; to trouble, afflict. (Now chiefly in interrogative or indefinite constructions.)
ain: noun. Alternative form of ayin (“Semitic letter”).
algid: adjective. Cold, chilly; used of low body temperature, especially in connection with certain diseases such as malaria and cholera.
algin: noun. Any of various gelatinous gums, derivatives of alginic acid, derived from marine brown algae and used especially as emulsifiers or
align: verb. To form a line; to fall into line.
and: conjunction. As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
ani: noun. Any bird of the genus Crotophaga in the cuckoo family.
anil: noun. The indigo shrub; or the indigo dye obtained from the plant.
dag: noun. A hanging end or shred, in particular a long pointed strip of cloth at the edge of a piece of clothing, or one of a row of decorative
dah: noun. The spoken representation of a dash in radio and telegraph Morse code.
dal: noun. Any of many dried husked pulses (legume), including peas, beans and lentils.
dial: noun. A graduated, circular scale over which a needle moves to show a measurement (such as speed).
dig: verb. To move hard-packed earth out of the way, especially downward to make a hole with a shovel.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
ding: noun. Very minor damage caused by being struck; a small dent or chip.
gad: interjection. An exclamation roughly equivalent to by God, goodness gracious, for goodness' sake.
gadi: noun. A cushioned throne used by Indian princes.
gal: noun. A young woman or girl; generally a form of rural Southern slang.
gid: noun. A disease caused by coenurosis of the brain, most commonly found in sheep and canids.
gild: verb. To cover with a thin layer of gold; to cover with gold leaf.
gin: noun. A colourless non - aged alcoholic liquor made by distilling fermented grains such as barley, corn, oats or rye with juniper berries;
gland: noun. A specialized cell, group of cells, or organ of endothelial origin in the human or animal body that synthesizes a chemical substance,
glia: noun. The network of glial cells that supports nervous system tissue.
had: verb. simple past and past participle of have
hading: verb. present participle and gerund of hade
hag: noun. A witch, sorceress, or enchantress; a female wizard.
hail: noun. Ball s or pieces of ice falling as precipitation, often in connection with a thunderstorm.
halid: noun. Any spider in the now obsolete family Halidae, which since 2006 is considered part of the family Pisauridae.
haling: verb. present participle and gerund of hale
hand: noun. The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
handing: verb. present participle and gerund of hand
handling: noun. A touching, controlling, managing, using, taking care of, etc., with the hand or hands, or as with the hands.
hang: verb. To be or remain suspended.
hin: noun. A former Hebrew liquid measure of volume (about 3.8 L).
hind: adjective. Located at the rear (most often said of animals' body parts).
inland: adjective. Within the land; relatively remote from the ocean or from open water; interior.
inn: noun. Any establishment where travellers can procure lodging, food, and drink.
lad: noun. A boy or young man.
laid: verb. simple past and past participle of lay
lain: verb. past participle of lie (“to be oriented in a horizontal position, situated”)
land: noun. The part of Earth which is not covered by oceans or other bodies of water.
landing: noun. An arrival at a surface, as of an airplane or any descending object.
liang: noun. Synonym of tael, a former Chinese unit of weight (about 40 g) and a related unit of silver currency.
lid: noun. The top or cover of a container.
ligand: noun. A molecule or moiety (ion, functional group, or radical) that binds to another chemical entity to form a larger complex; as,
lin: verb. To desist, to stop, to cease.
ling: noun. Any of various codlike saltwater fish often used as food by humans, those of the genus Molva, in the family Lotidae.
linn: noun. A waterfall or cataract (torrent of water running over a rocky bed), or a ravine down which such a waterfall rushes.
nag: noun. A small horse; a pony.
nail: noun. The thin, horny plate at the ends of fingers and toes on humans, chimpanzees and some other animals.
nan: noun. Synonym of maid: a servant girl.
nidal: adjective. Of or pertaining to nests.
adh: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adhola.
ahing: verb. present participle and gerund of ah
anding: noun. present participle and gerund of and
dali: noun. A timber tree (genus Myristica) of Guiana, whose wood is used for staves etc.
dan: noun. A title of honour or respect similar to "master" or "Sir", used of historical and legendary figures of the past.
dha: noun. Alternative form of dah (“Burmese knife”).
gali: noun. Alternative form of galee (“abuse; bad language”).
glid: verb. simple past and past participle of glide
hain: verb. To hedge or fence in; enclose; protect by hedging.
han: verb. plural simple present of have
hld: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Halang Doan.
ida: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Idakho-Isukha-Tiriki.
ing: noun. A meadow, especially a low meadow near a river; water meadow.
inga: noun. Any of the genus Inga of small tropical trees and shrubs.
lai: noun. A mostly North European medieval form of lyrical, narrative poem written in octosyllabic couplets that often deals with tales of
lan: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Laru.
lig: verb. To lie; be in a prostrate or recumbent position.
lind: noun. The lime tree, or linden tree.
linha: noun. A traditional short Portuguese unit of length, usually about equal to 2.3 mm.
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
nina: noun. A hidden message revealed in the completed grid of a crossword.
